sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
No calibrated nutrition, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, symptom, or metabolic model is supplied with this fixture.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis or diet recommendation.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, ancestry, diet history, environment, and non-genetic causes also matter.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated nutrition score, food-response model, micronutrient model, supplement model, symptom model, metabolic model, diet-history context, lab context, and ancestry applicability metadata unavailable.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context, but it should not prescribe foods, supplements, micronutrient targets, or metabolic conclusions without validated evidence.

nutritionContext: Use this report as educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a diet or supplement plan.
Track symptoms, diet history, labs, and health context with a clinician or registered dietitian when nutrition decisions matter.
Do not change foods, supplements, or medical care based on genotype alone.

references.title: Nutritional Genomics and Direct-to-Consumer Genetic Testing

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated nutrition, food-response, micronutrient, supplement, symptom, metabolic, diet-history, or lab model is supplied.
Other nutrition-related genetic and non-genetic contributors are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Nutrition traits and symptoms can be influenced by genetics, ancestry, diet history, environment, gut health, labs, medications, and other non-genetic factors.
A single marker should not be used to prescribe a diet, recommend supplements, or infer metabolic status.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not prescribe a diet, recommend supplements, set micronutrient targets, diagnose symptoms, or infer metabolic disease.
Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are still pending.
